events.text: Thomas Clark, 73-year-old Hispanic male, Former smoker (quit 5 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Adenocarcinoma of the Right upper lobe of lung. Clinical staging: T2aN2M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C34.11. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 elevated transaminases. ALT 95 U/L, AST 78 U/L. Treatment held one week, then resumed at reduced dose.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Underwent surgical resection: Right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. Estimated blood loss 150 mL. Length of stay 7 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant targeted therapy: Osimertinib 80 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on adjuvant targeted therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on adjuvant targeted therapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Date of Study: 2022-10-29

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Spiculated mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 5.4 x 4.3 cm. New finding.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 2.1 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, as described.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Specimen ID: S2022-38551
Date of Procedure: 2022-10-31
Date of Report: 2022-11-05

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.4 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Thomas Clark - Right upper lobe of lung" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 3.4 x 2.4 x 1.7 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Thoracic Surgery

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Date: 2022-11-01
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Clark is a 73-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, IIIA. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation. Pathology confirmed adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obstructive sleep apnea

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with colon cancer at age 70.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dizziness, night sweats, decreased appetite. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 148/73, HR 67, RR 18, Temp 97.8F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 93.3 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.8, Hgb 14.5, Plt 230, Cr 0.87

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
73-year-old male with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. initial oncology consultation.

1. Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Thoracic Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Date of Report: 2022-11-07
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.1 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 493x coverage.
Tumor content: 42%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

Patient: Thomas Clark

Date of Study: 2023-09-28

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 6 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 1.7 x 1.4 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.5 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Date of Study: 2024-09-07

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 8 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Spiculated mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 2.5 x 2.0 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.3 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology
